data.title: Germany's unemployment rate unchanged at 6.3% in November
data.description:
The unemployment rate in Germany remained unchanged at 6.3% in November.

data.title: Baidu to lay off staff across divisions after Q3 losses - report
data.description:
China's search engine giant Baidu (BIDU) started layoffs across multiple business units after reporting significant Q3 loss, Reuters reported, citing sources familiar with the matter.

The layoffs are expected to be larger scale and will likely run until the end of the current year. 

The layoffs are occurring across key divisions, including the mobile ecosystem group with some departments experiencing cuts up to 40%, the sources added. However, Baidu is protecting roles related to its AI and cloud initiatives and is reallocating resources to AI development.

The company posted an ~$1.6 billion loss for Q3, driven largely by asset write-downs and a decline in its core online advertising revenue.

data.title: Trump to halt migration from all 'Third World Countries' after National Guard shooting
data.description:
President Donald Trump has announced that he will permanently halt migration from all "Third World Countries," following a shooting near the White House that killed a National Guard member.

"I will permanently pause migration from all Third World Countries to allow the U.S. system to fully recover, terminate all of the millions of Biden illegal admissions, including those signed by Sleepy Joe Biden's Autopen, and remove anyone who is not a net asset to the United States, or is incapable of loving our Country, end all Federal benefits and subsidies to noncitizens of our Country, denaturalize migrants who undermine domestic tranquility, and deport any Foreign National who is a public charge, security risk, or non-compatible with Western Civilization," Trump posted on Truth Social.

The announcement came after two National Guardsmen were shot when they were patrolling near the White House on Wednesday. Sarah Beckstrom died of her injuries a day later, while Andrew Wolfe remains in critical condition.

Investigators said the shooting was carried out by an Afghan national, Rahmanullah Lakanwal, who was part of an Afghan partner force that worked closely with the CIA.

He later moved to the U.S. in 2021 under a Biden-era resettlement program for Afghans who were at risk and worked alongside U.S. forces in Afghanistan, following U.S. troops' withdrawal from the country.

Meanwhile, War Secretary Pete Hegseth said Trump requested that he order 500 additional National Guardsmen to Washington in the wake of the shooting.

The U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services issued new guidance "allowing for negative, country-specific factors to be considered when vetting aliens from 19 high-risk countries," including Afghanistan.

The USCIS stopped processing of all immigration requests relating to Afghan nationals, and its director Joseph Edlow ordered a "full scale, rigorous reexamination of every Green Card for every alien from every country of concern."

data.title: Spain's inflation expected to ease to 3% in November
data.description:
• The annual inflation rate in Spain eased to 3% in November 2025, from 3.1% in October, though it was above market estimates of 2.9%, according to preliminary estimates. The monthly CPI increased 0.20% in November of 2025 over the previous month.

  • Core consumer prices increased 2.60% in November over the same month in the previous year. 

  • Retail sales increased 3.8% in October over the same month in the previous year, lower than 4.2% during the previous month.

data.title: France economy expands 0.5% in Q3, inflation expected to stay steady
data.description:
• The Gross Domestic Product (GDP) in France expanded 0.50% in Q3 over the previous quarter, meeting estimates. GDP expanded 0.90% in Q3 over the same quarter of the previous year, 0.9%.
  • The inflation rate in France remained unchanged at 0.90% in November, lower than the consensus of 1%, preliminary estimates showed. The Consumer Price Index decreased 0.10% in November over the previous month.

data.title: Nexperia issues warning of industry-wide production disruptions, calls on China unit to reopen talks
data.description:
Auto chip maker Nexperia on Thursday warned that customers across industries are facing impending production halts while calling on its Chinese unit to take concrete steps to re-establish dialogue.

The Dutch chipmaker, which has lost the cooperation of its Chinese subsidiary since the Netherlands government took action to gain influence over decision-making, said in an open letter that it hasn’t received any meaningful response from its Chinese unit despite multiple attempts to re-establish dialogue.

It urged the Chinese entities to respond and take immediate steps to restore predictable and established supply flows without delay, adding that customers were “still reporting imminent production stoppages.”

“This is in the interest of all parties involved, including notably our customers,” Nexperia added.

The dispute began in September when Dutch Economic Affairs Minister Vincent Karremans used a Cold War-era law to assert control over Nexperia, owned by China’s Wingtech. The Dutch Ministry of Economic Affairs said it invoked the Goods Availability Act "due to serious governance shortcomings at semiconductor manufacturer Nexperia."

The Dutch government has since suspended its powers over Nexperia, restoring control to its Chinese parent company and easing a dispute with Beijing that had begun disrupting global automotive production.

data.title: Spetz announces name change to SonicStrategy
data.description:
• Spetz (OTCPK:DBKSF) announced official approval of its rebranding to reflect its focus as a dedicated validator infrastructure operator for the Sonic blockchain.
  • The company will file articles of amendment effective December 2, 2025 and complete its legal name change to "SonicStrategy Inc." 
  • In connection with the name change, the company will also change its ticker symbol on the Canadian Securities Exchange from "SPTZ" to "SONI".

data.title: Peter Thiel–backed drone maker Quantum Systems soars to €3B+ valuation with new €180M raise
data.description:
• Quantum Systems, a German drone maker backed by Peter Thiel, has raised €180 million in a Series C extension led by Balderton Capital, bringing its 2025 funding total to €340 million and tripling its valuation to more than €3 billion.
  • The funding represents the largest private raise in Europe’s dual-use sector this year, the company said.
  • The startup, whose new drone Jaeger is designed to intercept hostile unmanned aircraft, has seen demand surge following recent drone disruptions at major airports.
  • Quantum Systems was founded by former German army pilot Florian Seibel in 2015 to produce dual-use unmanned aerial vehicles. It’s backed by investors including Thiel, Porsche Automobil Holding SE (OTCPK:POAHY) and Notion Capital.

data.title: Petrobras trims five-year investment plan by 2% over low oil prices
data.description:
Brazilian state-controlled oil company Petrobras (PBR) trimmed its five-year investment plan for 2025-2029 by about 2%, reducing capital expenditures from $111 billion to $109 billion.

This move aims to protect its cash flow amid lower international oil prices. About $91 billion of the total will go to projects already under implementation, though $10 billion still requires budget confirmation pending financing analysis.

"Given the scenario of lower oil prices, the BP 2026-30 reinforces the commitment to growth with value generation and the company's financial sustainability through capital discipline, operational efficiency, optimization of operating expenses, and budget limits for investments, in addition to the adoption of more restrictive criteria in the governance for projects approvals" the company said in a statement.

The company is focused on maintaining financial stability without increasing debt or altering its dividend policy. This reduction to its multi-year plan is a first after President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva took office in 2023 and reflects balancing economic development goals with a challenging global oil price environment.

data.title: Germany's retail sales rise 0.9% Y/Y in October
data.description:
• Retail sales in Germany increased 0.90% in October over the same month in the previous year. 

  • Retail sales decreased 0.30% in October of 2025 over the previous month, above consensus of 0.20%.
